preamble: The shift from voiced dental stop [d] to the voiceless palatal stop [c] involves both palatalization and devoicing.

examples.note: During the early palatalization stages in Slavic, dental stops could devoice in specific positions while becoming palatalized.
Stages of palatalization in various Romance dialects often involved a devoiced palatal stop intermediate.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
